meta.description: Public-facing interactive environmental/species-records dashboard for BRERC (Bristol Regional Environmental Records Centre), delivered by 180 Degrees Consulting Bristol. This plan covers ONLY the React public front-end in web/ (Michael Samuel's scope). The backend API + PostgreSQL/PostGIS are owned within the 5-person team and appear here as a flagged dependency (assumptions[] + apiContract[]), never as Michael's build. R8 (internal dashboard) is a clearly deferred, secondary shell phase, conditional on team assignment of internal-web/.
meta.ownerScope: Public dashboard front-end only (the React app in web/). Not the api/ backend, not internal-web/ (R8) unless the team explicitly assigns it to Michael. Backend API + PostgreSQL/PostGIS are a dependency + flagged assumption. R8 appears only as a deferred, secondary phase whose front-end deliverables are conditional on both (a) R1-R7 Definition of Done being met and (b) the team assigning internal-web/ to Michael; otherwise P8 reduces to a spike + handover note to the internal-web/ owner.
meta.ownerHoursPerWeek: ASSUMED ~10-12 hours/week for one part-time student owner (A15). Phase sizing is back-solved against this: ~5 available working weeks between today (2026-07-08) and the final presentation (w/c 2026-08-10), i.e. roughly 50-60 owner-hours total for P0-P6.5, with P7 hardening + handover in the final-presentation..showcase week. If the real hours differ, phase depth (not the DoD gates) is what flexes — see timeline.deScopeDecisionTree.

meta.repoReality: Monorepo on branch michael/phase-0-foundations. web/ (Michael), api/ (team), internal-web/ (R8, secondary). GREENFIELD: no web/package.json yet; only scaffold dirs (web/src, web/public, web/tests). CLAUDE.md adopted; stray build artefacts removed. .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md ALREADY EXISTS (extend, do not recreate). Dependabot is ALREADY ACTIVE with open branches on web/ + api/ (incl. a node-26 bump vs ground-truth Node 25) — the first web/package.json commit will trigger a wave of PRs to triage (RK10). Team workflow: feature branches + PRs, Conventional Commits, no direct pushes to main.
meta.planningLens: Public-first (R1-R7 before R8); every phase/step traceable to a requirement id; dependsOn acyclic; realistic for one part-time front-end owner in ~5 working weeks. Data honesty + C2 (PII / sensitive-species) enforced by types, strict Zod, a CI grep-guard, contract tests, and e2e wire inspection — with server-side enforcement flagged as team-owned assumptions, and client-side checks framed as nets, not fixes.

requirements.title: Public-facing interactive live dashboard hosted on BRERC's website
requirements.notes: PRIMARY, centre of gravity. Self-contained statically-served SPA / embeddable bundle with a documented build output. Everything else serves this; public-first ordering is mandatory.

requirements.title: Interactive species distribution maps (grid-reference aware)
requirements.notes: Likely centrepiece. MapLibre GL JS via react-map-gl/maplibre. Honest grid/atlas cells at true precision, NOT false-precision pins. Data reprojected 27700->4326 server-side; client renders WGS84 (4326) only.

requirements.title: Species information + attributed/licensed images
requirements.notes: e.g. Wikipedia/Wikimedia CC BY-SA / PD imagery, with visible attribution + licence and meaningful alt text. Covers species AND geodiversity where in scope.

requirements.title: Engaging, user-friendly, visually appealing UX for non-expert public
requirements.notes: Colour, fonts, navigation. Design tokens centralise colour so contrast is auditable (ties to R5). Product/UX/visual decisions defer to the Strategy prompt.

requirements.title: Accessibility WCAG 2.2 AA + published accessibility statement
requirements.notes: LEGAL build-blocker. Automated axe AND manual keyboard/screen-reader pass. Non-map fallback (accessible table/summary) mandatory. A cross on R5 blocks merge.

requirements.title: Live PostgreSQL; dev subset now -> prod credential swap, config-only
requirements.notes: The credential swap lives entirely in the API environment. The client only knows an API base URL (+ map style/tile URLs) via VITE_* (public). No DB credentials, tokens, or SQL ever in the client. dev->prod is not a client code change.

requirements.title: Fully documented + commented source + technical docs for handover
requirements.notes: README, .env.example, ADRs, handover runbook, accessibility statement, why-not-what comments (esp. grid-ref/projection + sensitivity logic). Deliverable.

requirements.title: Internal staff monitoring dashboard (summary stats + data-quality checks + reporting)
requirements.notes: SECONDARY. Only if R1-R7 DoD is met, time permits, AND the team assigns internal-web/ to Michael. Summary stats over records/species/Recorder1/years/ dates/BLISS; filtering; data-quality checks (invalid grid refs, bad dates, carriage returns, missing values, duplicates); reporting. Lives in internal-web/; represented here only as a deferred shell/spike phase.

requirements.title: 8-week public-first scope, refined with BRERC
requirements.notes: Brief lists POTENTIAL work — candidates, not commitments. Scope refined WITH Tim Corner; never invent requirements; never treat feature ideas as fixed.

requirements.title: Data ethics — sensitive species + recorder PII/GDPR
requirements.notes: HARD STOP if violated. Sensitive-species precise coords generalised/suppressed server-side and made INDISTINGUISHABLE (no sensitivity marker in public payloads); Recorder1/PII never reach the client; Comments withheld entirely from public; Place suppressed for sensitive taxa; low-count re-identification bucketed server-side. A client-side-only fix = a contract bug to flag. A cross blocks merge.

requirements.title: Maintainable by BRERC staff post-handover
requirements.notes: Boring, well-understood patterns; no hidden magic; documented decisions; docs current in-repo. Partner to R7.

assumptions.statement: A team-owned backend API exists (or will exist) exposing HTTPS/JSON and/or vector-tile endpoints at a single base URL read from config. The React client never connects to PostgreSQL and holds no DB credentials.
assumptions.why_it_matters: The entire front-end data layer (lib/api), the apiContract, and the MSW mock are built against this boundary. If the API is late, the front-end proceeds against the MSW mock and swaps only VITE_API_BASE_URL at integration (P6.5).

assumptions.statement: The API reprojects EPSG:27700 (Eastings/Northings) -> EPSG:4326 (WGS84) via ST_Transform server-side and returns WGS84 grid-cell geometry + precision, never raw BNG eastings/northings or false-precision points.
assumptions.why_it_matters: MapLibre renders Web Mercator and needs 4326; raw BNG is both a rendering bug and (at full precision) a C2 location leak.

assumptions.statement: The API strips Recorder1 and all recorder PII, and WITHHOLDS the free-text Comments field entirely from public responses (no sanitise-and-ship path), server-side, before any public response leaves the API.
assumptions.why_it_matters: C2 HARD STOP + GDPR. Recorder1/BLISS are named in the full DB but ABSENT from the sample; the contract must EXPLICITLY exclude them and never assume sample-absence means production-absence. Comments (25-80% populated) may embed PII/site detail and free-text sanitisation is unreliable and client-unverifiable — so public = withheld.

assumptions.statement: The API generalises (coarser grid) OR suppresses precise coordinates for sensitive/protected taxa server-side, blending them INTO the ordinary grid so they are INDISTINGUISHABLE from normal records — enforced in what the tile/GeoJSON endpoint RETURNS, with NO per-feature sensitivity marker in any public payload, and Place suppressed to null for sensitive taxa.
assumptions.why_it_matters: C2 HARD STOP. A client-side hide of downloaded precise coords is still a breach, and a 'sensitivity: generalised' label is itself a re-identification vector (an attacker enumerates the flagged cells). NBN Atlas/GBIF practice blends, never labels. A free-text Place defeats grid coarsening.
assumptions.confirm_with: Team (backend/API owner) + Tim Corner (definitive sensitive-taxa list)

assumptions.statement: The definitive list of sensitive/protected taxa and the generalisation resolution per tier (e.g. 1km / 10km) are provided by BRERC and enforced server-side. A global public precision FLOOR is applied so no public cell is finer than the coarsest tier any sensitive taxon could require, so the client need not know which taxa are sensitive.
assumptions.why_it_matters: The client cannot decide sensitivity and cannot verify per-taxon precision without knowing the sensitive list; a server-enforced global floor makes correctness server-owned. Drives map max-zoom cap, legend precision language, honesty copy.

assumptions.statement: For large record sets the API serves vector tiles (PostGIS ST_AsMVT via a tile endpoint / pg_tileserv / Martin); for small/filtered sets it serves GeoJSON suitable for MapLibre native clustering. Both are already sensitivity-generalised + reprojected.
assumptions.why_it_matters: Determines whether the map source is a vector-tile source or a GeoJSON source and drives payload budgets (CLAUDE.md §6).

assumptions.statement: A map style JSON + basemap tile URLs (self-hostable or licensed) are available and CSP-compatible; the client reads them from non-secret VITE_* config and holds no map-provider secrets; required basemap attribution is available to render.
assumptions.why_it_matters: MapLibre needs a style/tile source; CSP must permit those hosts; any provider key must sit server-side, not as a client secret (R6). Basemap attribution is a legal duty.
assumptions.confirm_with: Team + Tim Corner / BRERC hosting (basemap licensing for a public gov site)

assumptions.statement: Species imagery + licence/attribution metadata (author, licence, licenceUrl, sourceUrl) come from an approved, correctly-licensed source (e.g. Wikipedia/Wikimedia CC BY-SA / PD) via the API or a documented CSP-allow-listed origin, returned as data.
assumptions.why_it_matters: R3 requires visible attribution; unattributed CC BY-SA use is a licence breach. Attribution must be structured data, not hard-coded; the source path affects CSP.
assumptions.confirm_with: Tim Corner + team (approved image source + attribution policy)

assumptions.statement: The dashboard is embedded/hosted on BRERC's existing website as a static SPA / embeddable bundle; the embedding method (iframe vs mounted bundle vs sub-path), base path, routing mode (hash vs history), and parent-page CSP are provided by BRERC.
assumptions.why_it_matters: R1 hosting. The embedding method affects routing, CSP, CORS allow-list, focus management, responsive sizing, and the build output shape.

assumptions.statement: The dev subset shape (19-column sample below) is representative of the public-facing fields; the production main DB adds Recorder1 and BLISS which the public contract excludes. Temporal precision for public display/filter may be coarsened (prefer year/ YearEnd over exact datetime) where it reduces re-identification risk.
assumptions.why_it_matters: Fixtures + Zod schemas are drafted from this shape; divergence at prod swap must not break the client. Temporal precision can aid re-identification of recorders/sites.

assumptions.statement: Until the real API is ready, the front-end develops against a local MSW mock implementing the proposed apiContract with fixtures shaped from the sample; the mock is a dev/test aid, never shipped, and is converged onto the real contract early. All C2/R6 claims proven against the mock are RE-PROVEN against the real API at integration (P6.5).
assumptions.why_it_matters: Decouples the sole front-end owner from the team-backend dependency and keeps R1-R5 progressing. But mock-only proof is circular (the mock is built to pass its own contract), so a real-API integration gate is mandatory before showcase.

assumptions.statement: dev and prod differ ONLY by API environment (DB credentials swapped inside the API). The client's only environment difference is VITE_API_BASE_URL (+ map style/tile URLs); no client code branches on environment.
assumptions.why_it_matters: R6 — the credential swap must be config-only. Any client env branch would violate this.

assumptions.statement: Geodiversity records (not only species) may be in public scope; if confirmed, they share the records/map contract shape unless the team specifies otherwise.
assumptions.why_it_matters: Scientific-honesty note: geodiversity is named in scope. If in scope it needs a layer/type; if not, it stays out to protect C1.

assumptions.statement: The 'group' dimension (e.g. reptiles / geodiversity) used for browse/filter is DERIVED server-side from a taxonomy/classification mapping, NOT a column in the 19-column sample (which has only Scientific_Name / Common_Name). The classification source and permitted value set are team-/BRERC-owned.
assumptions.why_it_matters: Anti-fabrication: every other derived field (recordCount, firstYear/lastYear, verifiedCount, hasImage) is trivially derivable from sample columns, but 'group' needs an external taxonomy the sample does not contain. Ties to A13/Q9 (geodiversity).

assumptions.statement: One part-time student owner has ~10-12 productive hours/week on this front-end, i.e. ~50-60 hours across the ~5 working weeks from today to the final presentation, with the final-presentation..showcase week reserved for hardening/handover.
assumptions.why_it_matters: Phase sizing and the mid-review de-scope depend on a real hours budget; without it, 'feasible for one part-time owner' is unbacked. Drives the P2 spike de-scope + buffers.

assumptions.statement: The actual reptile/varied sample export (main5.xls rows) is NOT in the repo. Fixtures and Zod field typing are hand-authored from the documented 19-column schema (varying Grid_Ref precision, sometimes-non-numeric Abundance, en-dash in 'verified', etc.), not read from the file. Access to the real export would improve fixture realism.
assumptions.why_it_matters: A handover reader must not hunt for main5.xls in the repo; fixture realism rests on the documented schema. If the real export is obtainable, fixtures should be re-derived.
assumptions.confirm_with: Team + Tim Corner (share the dev subset export)

assumptions.statement: A team member owns internal-web/ (R8). If it is not assigned to Michael, P8 reduces to a spike + a handover note rather than committed internal-web/ front-end files.
assumptions.why_it_matters: meta.ownerScope is web/ only; committing internal-web/ files under Michael's name would blur the scope boundary and risk the public-first breach RK7 warns about.

groundTruthData.sampleTable: varied / reptile sample from main5.xls (dev subset shape; file not in repo, A16)

fields.name: Scientific_Name
fields.notes: Species-level key for lookups (R3), search, and map filtering (R2). Public speciesId is derived from this server-side.

fields.name: Grid_Ref
fields.kind: string OS BNG, VARYING precision ('ST6674588734'=1m/10-fig vs 'ST498705'=100m/6-fig)
fields.notes: Precision must be legible as honest grid cells (R2). Fine (1m/10-fig) refs are a location leak — generalise/suppress server-side to the tier floor (C2/A4/A5). Client shows a cell at the RETURNED precision; gridRef string precision MUST equal precisionMetres and is never up-sampled client-side to a false-precision pin.

fields.name: Place
fields.notes: Free-text locality can pinpoint a sensitive site and DEFEAT grid coarsening. For sensitive taxa it MUST be suppressed (null) server-side; for public it is coarsened to a named-area/parish level, never exact street locality. Not returned finer than the geometry's precision (C2/A4).

fields.name: Date_of_Record
fields.notes: Temporal; parse defensively (may be absent/vague/malformed). Coarsen to year for public where it reduces re-identification (A10).

fields.name: Abundance
fields.notes: Count of the record, NOT population size (records != populations caveat). Not always numeric — parse defensively.

fields.name: Precise_Date
fields.notes: Exact datetime — coarsen for public (A10). Handle nulls.

fields.name: Vague_Date
fields.notes: Nulls are normal; render 'date range' honestly.

fields.name: verified
fields.kind: string ('Accepted – correct' — note the non-ASCII en-dash)
fields.notes: Useful for a data-confidence signal (R4). DEFENSIVELY normalise/whitelist (handle the en-dash and unexpected variants) in lib/api and derive a boolean/enum, rather than string-equality against a literal (mirrors the R8 carriage-return/bad-date hazards).

fields.name: YearEnd
fields.notes: Preferred coarse temporal axis for public filtering/slider (R2/A10).

fields.name: Comments
fields.notes: MAY EMBED PII/site detail. WITHHELD ENTIRELY from the public client (C2/A3) — no sanitise-and-ship path, since free-text sanitisation is unreliable and client-unverifiable, and no R-requirement displays Comments publicly. Any sanitisation-and-display belongs only to internal-web/ (R8, auth-gated).

fields.name: unique_No
fields.notes: Stable opaque per-RECORD id for record detail views; not PII. Distinct from the derived per-SPECIES speciesId.

fields.name: licence
fields.notes: Data licence for the record (distinct from image licence, R3/A8). Do not assume a licence where absent.

fields.name: Eastings
fields.notes: PRECISE-LOCATION LEAK. Never sent to the client (A2/C2). API reprojects to 4326 and returns only geometry at the permitted precision.

fields.name: Northings
fields.notes: PRECISE-LOCATION LEAK. Same rule as Eastings.

fields.name: Recorder1
fields.kind: string (PII) — NAMED IN BRIEF, ABSENT FROM SAMPLE
fields.notes: Recorder personal data. Named in the full main DB, not in this sample. The public contract EXPLICITLY excludes it; contract tests assert its absence. Never assume it is already gone (C2/A3).

fields.name: BLISS
fields.kind: source-database field — NAMED IN BRIEF, ABSENT FROM SAMPLE
fields.notes: Internal source-DB metadata; excluded from the public contract. Relevant to R8 internal, not public.

sensitivityRules: STRIP (never in any public client payload): Recorder1, BLISS, Eastings, Northings, Comments (withheld entirely) — contract-test + CI grep-guard enforced every build.

NO sensitivity marker: public payloads carry NO per-feature/row 'sensitivity' flag; sensitive records are blended INTO the ordinary grid, indistinguishable from normal ones (A4). 'sensitivity' is on the grep-guard forbidden list for public payloads.

GENERALISE/SUPPRESS server-side for sensitive/protected taxa: Grid_Ref precision + geometry, Place (suppress to null / coarsen to named-area), Precise_Date — enforced in what the endpoint RETURNS (A4), not by a client hide.

PRECISION FLOOR (A5): no public cell is finer than the coarsest sensitive tier any sensitive taxon could require; correctness is server-owned (the client cannot verify per-taxon sensitivity). Place is never returned finer than the cell geometry.

LOW-COUNT SUPPRESSION (k-anonymity): per-cell recordCount/verifiedCount for rare/sensitive taxa are bucketed (e.g. '<5') or suppressed below a threshold server-side, so a generalised cell with count 1 + year + place cannot isolate a single observation.
COARSEN temporal for public where it reduces re-identification (prefer YearEnd/year over exact datetime) (A10).

CONSISTENCY: the records-table gridRef precision EQUALS the map cell precisionMetres for the same taxon/filter; the accessible fallback never leaks finer location than the map; lib/geo never up-samples a coarse ref.
REVIEW server-side: licence — inspected for correctness; never assume a licence where the field is NULL.
Client renders WGS84 (4326) honest grid cells at the RETURNED precision — never a false-precision pin; reprojection from 27700 is server-side (A2).
PUBLIC: everything else, delivered honestly with precision + verification status. Records != populations; recorder/spatial/temporal effort bias is the central caveat surfaced in UI (R4/§6).

stack.framework: React + TypeScript (strict, no any — use unknown + narrowing)
stack.maps: MapLibre GL JS via react-map-gl/maplibre (imperative calls isolated behind hooks/refs in features/map)
stack.a11yPrimitives: React Aria / React Aria Components (menus, dialogs, selects, tabs, sliders, tables)
stack.serverState: TanStack Query (staleTime, retry, loading/error/empty) — no fetch-in-useEffect scattered in components
stack.boundaryValidation: Zod at the API boundary — bad data fails loudly
stack.mockLayer: MSW (Mock Service Worker) implementing the apiContract (A11) — dev/test only, never shipped
stack.packageManager: npm (Node 25 per ground truth; no pnpm/yarn; Dependabot pinned to Node 25 not 26)

monorepo.internal-web: R8 internal dashboard (secondary; deferred; owner TBD per A17)

webSrc.app/: app shell, routing, providers (QueryClient, map, a11y, error boundaries)
webSrc.features/about/: data provenance, honesty caveats, accessibility statement, attributions (R5/R7/R3)
webSrc.components/: shared, presentational, accessible primitives (React Aria based, R4/R5)
webSrc.lib/api/: the ONLY place that calls the backend (typed client + Zod)
webSrc.lib/geo/: grid-ref precision/label utilities, tile config (tested; display-only, NO client BNG->WGS84 reprojection, never up-samples precision)
webSrc.types/: shared domain types (Species, Record, GridCell...) — reused, not duplicated
webSrc.config/: env-driven config (API base URL, map style, tile URLs) reading import.meta.env

apiBoundary.rule: Exactly ONE API layer (web/src/lib/api). No fetch scattered in components. Client never connects to PostgreSQL, holds no DB credentials, and never assembles SQL.
apiBoundary.validation: Every response parsed through a Zod schema in lib/api before it reaches a component; a parse failure surfaces as an error state, not a silent crash. Domain types live in types/ and are shared/reused.
apiBoundary.serverStateFetching: TanStack Query wraps the typed api functions (staleTime/retry/loading/error) — never fetch-in-useEffect.

dataDelivery.largeSets: Server MVT vector tiles (ST_AsMVT) — sensitivity-generalised (blended, unlabelled) + reprojected server-side (A6).
dataDelivery.honestDefault: Grid/atlas occupancy polygons at the record's true precision — never coarse records as exact pins.
dataDelivery.projection: Data ships in WGS84 (4326); reprojection from EPSG:27700 is server-side (A2). lib/geo holds tested grid-ref precision/label helpers for display only, never to fabricate precision.
dataDelivery.sensitivity: Coarsening/suppression enforced in what the tile/GeoJSON endpoint RETURNS (A4); sensitive records blended into the ordinary grid with NO marker; a global precision floor applies (A5).
dataDelivery.performance: Cap max zoom to meaningful precision; debounce viewport-driven fetches; simplify geometry; lazy-load the map bundle; budget payloads.

envConfig.source: import.meta.env (VITE_* is PUBLIC — client knows only non-secret URLs)
envConfig.credentialSwap: R6 dev->prod is a credential change INSIDE the API environment (A12). The client changes only VITE_API_BASE_URL — never code. Both env sets documented in .env.example + the handover runbook; real values never committed. Proven config-only at P6.5/P7.

apiContract.note: PROPOSED and FLAGGED AS ASSUMPTION (A1). This is the contract the FRONT-END needs; the team's API owner confirms/implements/owns it. The front-end develops against MSW mock fixtures of these exact shapes (A11) and converges early; every claim is RE-PROVEN against the real API at integration (P6.5). Every response is Zod-validated in lib/api. Every endpoint EXCLUDES Recorder1, BLISS, Eastings, Northings, Comments, and any 'sensitivity' marker from public payloads (C2). Sensitive records are blended into the ordinary grid (no marker). MVT is binary — validated by a contract test on a decoded fixture rather than runtime Zod.

endpoints.purpose: Liveness/readiness probe so the client can show a graceful degraded state if the API is down.
Searchable, paginated species/taxa index for search/browse and the species index (R3).
endpoints.requestParams: q?: string; group?: string (DERIVED server-side, A14); page?: int; pageSize?: int (server-capped); sort?: whitelisted enum
endpoints.responseShape: { items: Array<{ speciesId: string, scientificName, commonName, group, recordCount, firstYear, lastYear, hasImage: boolean }>, page, pageSize, total } — speciesId is a server-derived per-taxon id (A14); recordCount bucketed for rare/sensitive taxa

endpoints.purpose: Species detail: description + attributed/licensed image + honest summary stats (R3).
endpoints.requestParams: path: speciesId (server-derived per-taxon id, A14; whitelisted/validated server-side)
endpoints.responseShape: { speciesId, scientificName, commonName, group, description?, image?: { url, author, licence, licenceUrl, sourceUrl, alt }, stats: { recordCount, yearRange: [int,int], verifiedCount } }

endpoints.purpose: Vector tiles of occupancy grid cells for large record sets (R2). Sensitivity generalisation already applied + blended server-side.
endpoints.requestParams: path z/x/y; query speciesId?, yearFrom?, yearTo? (server-validated/whitelisted; bbox/zoom capped)
endpoints.responseShape: MVT layer 'cells' with feature props { cellId, precisionMetres, recordCount (bucketed for rare taxa), speciesId? } — geometry WGS84 cell polygon; NO precise points, NO eastings/northings, NO preciseGridRef, NO sensitivity marker, NO place

endpoints.purpose: GeoJSON occupancy cells for small/filtered sets — MapLibre clustering + the non-map accessible table (R2/R5).
endpoints.responseShape: GeoJSON FeatureCollection; each Feature.properties = { cellId, precisionMetres, recordCount (bucketed for rare taxa), verifiedCount (bucketed) }; geometry = WGS84 cell polygon; NO sensitivity marker; NO place for sensitive taxa (suppressed server-side, A4)

endpoints.purpose: Paginated, sanitised record rows behind a selected cell/filter/viewport — powers the NON-MAP accessible data table (R5) and cell detail.
endpoints.requestParams: gridRef or bbox; speciesId?; yearFrom?; yearTo?; page?; pageSize? (server-capped)
endpoints.responseShape: { items: Array<{ id, scientificName, commonName, gridRef (generalised; precision EQUALS precisionMetres), precisionMetres, place? (suppressed/null for sensitive taxa; never finer than geometry), year, abundance?, recordType?, verified (normalised enum), source }>, page, pageSize, total }

endpoints.purpose: Aggregate counts + honest bias framing for the R1 landing dashboard.
endpoints.responseShape: { totalRecords, totalSpecies, yearRange: {min,max}, recordsByYear: Array<{year,count}>, topGroups: Array<{group,count}>, coverageCaveat: string }

endpoints.purpose: Data provenance, coverage caveats (effort/spatial/temporal bias), sources, last-updated, declared sensitivity policy summary + attributions — for the About/provenance page (R7/R4/C2).
endpoints.responseShape: { lastUpdated, recordTotal, sources: string[], coverageCaveats: string[], sensitivityPolicy: { generalisationTiersMetres: number[], appliesToProtectedTaxa: true, note: string }, attributions: Array<{ label, url, licence }> } — a POLICY summary only (transparency), never per-record sensitivity data

phases.name: Foundations, guardrails, design tokens & green CI
phases.goal: Stand up the greenfield web/ Vite+React+TS(strict) app with tooling, env-driven config, design tokens + the shared React Aria primitive library, and the C2/a11y guardrails wired in FIRST, behind a permanently-green CI gate. Pulling tokens + primitives here (not P2) means the mid-review slice is ASSEMBLY, not foundation-building (fixes the P0-P2 compression blocker). No feature logic yet. Critical-path enabler.
deliverables: web/ Vite + React + TypeScript(strict) app that builds and serves
CLAUDE.md §4 folder layout with a purpose note per folder
ESLint (typescript-eslint + jsx-a11y) + Prettier; strict tsconfig; npm scripts (dev/build/preview/typecheck/lint/test/test:e2e)
Vitest + RTL + jest-axe wired (one green smoke); Playwright + @axe-core/playwright installed (one green smoke)
CI pipeline (typecheck + lint + unit + jest-axe + C2/secret grep-guard) on every PR; no merge on red
config/ env module (Zod-validated) + .env.example documenting VITE_API_BASE_URL/MAP_STYLE_URL/TILE_URL (dev+prod, values omitted)
Accessible app shell (skip-link, landmarks, visible focus, reduced-motion helper, per-feature error boundary, QueryClientProvider)
Design tokens (colour/type/spacing, AA-auditable) + shared React Aria primitives (Button, Select, Dialog, Tabs, Table) + LoadingState/ErrorState/EmptyState
Dependabot config scoped/pinned to Node 25 (not 26), grouped + scheduled to reduce single-owner triage churn

README (setup/run/build/test); ADR-0001 (stack + one-API-layer boundary); ADR-0002 (env/config + config-only credential swap); ADR-0004 (design tokens); extended .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md (requirement ID + a11y note + test evidence)
exitCriteria: npm run build, typecheck, lint, test all green locally and in CI; strict TS on, no any; jsx-a11y active

steps.description: Provenance page from useProvenance: sources, last-updated, and the central effort/spatial/temporal-bias + records!=populations caveats; geodiversity-inclusive if in scope (A13). Consolidated attributions for imagery, basemap, and data licences; a declared sensitivity-POLICY summary (generalisation tiers + transparency note) — NOT per-record sensitivity data and NOT a client enforcement mechanism. Do not assume a licence where absent (licence col mostly NULL).
acceptance: bias caveats visible; all attributions correct + licensed; no PII/sensitive per-record detail surfaced; sensitivity summary is policy-level only
tests: render + a11y test; attribution-presence test
risks: Missing an attribution = licence breach; geodiversity scope + caveat wording unconfirmed — flag to Tim Corner (A8/A13/RK8)

phases.name: Real-API integration & C2 re-proof against real backend
phases.goal: Close the mock-only circularity: point the client at the REAL team API (or staging), reconcile Zod schema drift, RE-PROVE the C2 wire gates against REAL responses (no PII/precise-coords/ sensitivity marker; precision floor met; Place suppressed for sensitive taxa; Comments absent), and confirm the R6 dev->prod swap was code-free. This is the one place the 'config-only swap' and 'no precise coords on the wire' claims are validated against reality rather than the self-built mock.
deliverables: Client pointed at the real/staging API via VITE_API_BASE_URL only (no code change) — swap evidence recorded
Zod schema drift reconciled against real responses; strict-vs-passthrough decision confirmed
C2 wire-inspection e2e RE-RUN against REAL responses (PII/precise-coords/sensitivity-marker absence; precision floor; Place suppression; Comments absence)
A dated dependency note: the real (or staging) API must exist by this integration date for it to complete before showcase
exitCriteria: The dev->prod repoint proven CONFIG-ONLY: only VITE_API_BASE_URL changed, no client code diff (R6 gate)
C2 wire gates PASS against REAL API responses, not just the mock (C2 gate — blocks merge); any leak escalated to the team as a server-contract bug (HARD STOP)
Zod schemas reconciled with the real contract; no silent coercion of unexpected/leaked keys
If the real/staging API is unavailable by the integration date, this is flagged as the top schedule risk (RK1) and a fallback slice + contingency stated

steps.title: Point at real API, reconcile Zod drift, re-prove C2 wire gates
steps.description: Swap VITE_API_BASE_URL to the real/staging endpoint (no code change), run the full suite, reconcile any Zod schema drift (RK13), and RE-RUN the C2 wire-inspection e2e against REAL responses. Record swap evidence (git diff shows only env change) in the handover runbook. Escalate any real-response C2 leak immediately as a server-contract bug.
acceptance: only VITE_API_BASE_URL changed for the repoint (config-only, R6)
C2 wire gates green against REAL responses; drift reconciled
tests: integration e2e against real/staging API; env-only-diff assertion
risks: Backend late/divergent (RK1, high) — keep MSW path working, state the required API-ready date, fall back to mock demo if unavailable; real-response C2 leak = HARD STOP (RK2)

phases.name: Hardening — perf budget, E2E + a11y automation, resilience, docs & handover
phases.goal: Bring the public dashboard to full Definition of Done for the final presentation and showcase: perf budgets met, Playwright + axe journeys (incl. keyboard-only) in CI, resilient error/ telemetry handling, a documented static build + CSP/embed guidance, and the complete handover pack — so R1-R7 are demonstrably shipped before any R8 work is considered.
deliverables: Playwright + @axe-core/playwright E2E for core journeys (load -> map -> species -> filter) incl. keyboard-only; wired as a CI gate
Completed per-feature manual keyboard/screen-reader audit checklist (incl. the mid-review spike's deferred items)
Bundle + payload budgets set + checked in CI; code-splitting/lazy-map verified; Core Web Vitals (LCP/INP/CLS) measured on a modest-device profile; long lists/tables virtualised
Resilient error/telemetry handling: per-feature boundaries; a client-error hook that never leaks PII/sensitive detail
Documented static build output + CSP guidance compatible with MapLibre style/tile/image sources; CORS + rate-limit recommendations flagged as server-owned
Handover pack: README, .env.example (dev+prod), ADR index, handover runbook (prod repoint, tile/API config, publishing the accessibility statement)
exitCriteria: E2E journey load->map->species->filter passes in CI, including keyboard-only; axe clean across routes; manual a11y checklist complete incl. deferred spike items (R5 gate — no cross)
Within perf/bundle budget on modest devices; map + heavy libs lazy-loaded; CWV measured (perf DoD)
e2e network inspection confirms zero PII/precise-coords/BNG/sensitivity-marker on the wire (C2 gate); error logs carry no sensitive detail
Handover runbook proves the R6 dev->prod repoint is config-only (apiBaseUrl swap, no client code change), consistent with P6.5 evidence (R6/C3/R7 gate)
Documented static build embeds on a BRERC-like host; CSP permits map sources; routing works when embedded (A9)

steps.title: E2E + a11y automation + manual checklist (incl. deferred spike items)
steps.description: Playwright covers load -> explore map -> open species -> filter, with a keyboard-only variant, plus @axe-core/playwright scans across key routes; assert no keyboard traps + logical focus order. Complete and record the per-feature manual keyboard + screen-reader pass, INCLUDING any items the mid-review spike (P2) deferred (axe catches ~1/3; manual is mandatory).
acceptance: core journey + keyboard-only journey green in CI; axe clean on key routes; manual SR pass recorded incl. deferred items
no keyboard trap on any route
tests: the Playwright + axe specs themselves
risks: Automated axe catches ~1/3 — manual pass is non-negotiable (§8); flaky map e2e — stabilise with deterministic fixtures (RK4/RK14)

steps.title: Performance budgets + code-splitting + resilience/telemetry
steps.description: Set bundle/payload budgets checked in CI; verify route/feature code-splitting and the lazy map load; analyse the bundle; measure LCP/INP/CLS on a throttled modest-device profile; virtualise long tables. Harden loading/error/empty/partial-data paths; per-feature boundaries; a client-error logging hook that redacts by default and never leaks PII/sensitive detail.
acceptance: budgets defined + checked; map + heavy libs not in the initial chunk; CWV within target
one feature failing never blanks the page; error logs carry no PII/sensitive detail
tests: bundle-size check + lazy-load assertion in CI; Lighthouse/CWV spot check
fault-injection e2e; telemetry-redaction unit test
risks: MapLibre is heavy — must stay lazy or it blows LCP (RK6); telemetry leaking detail — redact by default (RK6)

steps.title: Handover pack + static build + CSP/embed guidance (R6 repoint)
steps.description: Complete the handover for a BRERC dev who has never met the team: README (setup/env/run/build/deploy/ embed/test), .env.example (dev+prod, values omitted), ADR index, and a runbook covering the config-only dev->prod repoint (apiBaseUrl swap, no code change; consistent with P6.5 evidence), tile/API config, and publishing the accessibility statement. Finalise the self-contained static bundle; document a CSP compatible with MapLibre style/tile/image sources; note CORS (known origins) + rate-limit recommendations as server-owned. Confirm embed model + routing (A9).
acceptance: a fresh dev can build/run/point-at-prod/deploy from docs alone; the prod repoint is config-only (no code change)
static build embeds on a BRERC-like host; CSP permits map sources; routing works when embedded
tests: docs lint / link check; clean-checkout build-from-README smoke
build + serve static output; verify map + CSP in a staging embed
risks: Stale docs are worse than none — keep in-repo and current (§13/RK8); embedding constraints unknown — resolve A9 before final (RK5)

phases.name: SECONDARY / DEFERRED — internal staff monitoring dashboard shell (R8)
phases.goal: ONLY after R1-R7 DoD is fully met (P7 exit criteria all green), time remains, AND the team assigns internal-web/ to Michael (A17): begin a minimal internal-web/ shell (summary stats + one data-quality check) reusing web/ patterns. If not assigned, this reduces to a spike + handover note to the internal-web/ owner. Explicitly deferred; never at the expense of the public dashboard.
deliverables: IF assigned (A17): internal-web/ shell reusing web/ toolchain + shared components + a11y where safe (same DoD gates); OTHERWISE: a spike + handover note to the internal-web/ owner
Read-only summary stats (records/species/years/dates/BLISS internal context) + filtering, against a team-owned auth-gated internal endpoint
Data-quality checks surface (invalid grid refs, bad dates, carriage returns, missing values, duplicates), access-controlled by the team
exitCriteria: Entered ONLY if P7 exit criteria (R1-R7 DoD) are fully green AND internal-web/ is assigned to Michael (A17) — otherwise reduces to a spike/handover note (C1/R1 gate)

No public-dashboard regression caused by starting this; still no client DB access; internal PII (Recorder1/BLISS/Comments) handled per team auth policy, NOT inherited from the public contract, never leaking to the public build (C2)
Same DoD gates (typed/accessible/tested/documented) apply to any internal-web/ code committed

steps.title: Internal shell scaffold + summary stats + one data-quality check (gated on R1-R7 done + assignment)
steps.description: IF internal-web/ is assigned to Michael (A17) and P7 is green: scaffold internal-web/ reusing public toolchain/components; do NOT reuse the public PII-stripping contract blindly — internal views may need different (authorised, auth-gated) fields. Render read-only summary stats + one data-quality check (e.g. invalid grid refs) against a flagged internal API. Data-quality logic is team-owned. OTHERWISE: produce a spike + handover note for the internal-web/ owner. Explicitly deferred: no work here until the public dashboard's DoD is met.
acceptance: only started after P7 is green AND assigned (A17); reuses web/ patterns; does not touch the public build
internal data access is auth-gated (server-owned, flagged); data-quality checks present
tests: smoke build + a11y baseline; data-quality rule detection unit test
risks: R8 cannibalising public-dashboard time — hard stop per C1/R1; internal dashboard may expose Recorder1/Comments to staff — must be explicitly authorised, access-controlled, logged (RK7/C2); scope boundary — reduce to handover note if unassigned (A17)

accessibility: WCAG 2.2 AA is a build-blocker on every phase (except the explicitly-exempt mid-review spike P2, whose deferred items are cleared in P3-S3/P7-S1): semantic HTML, keyboard operability (incl. the map), visible focus, correct ARIA, non-map fallback (R5).
Build on React Aria / React Aria Components for interactive widgets; keep native semantics, add ARIA only to fill gaps — never reinvent accessible widgets.
jest-axe in component tests + @axe-core/playwright in E2E + a MANUAL keyboard/screen-reader pass per feature (automated catches ~1/3).
Never encode meaning by colour alone (map legend + status): also shape/label/pattern; AA contrast from documented tokens; resizable text; no info lost at 200% zoom.
Non-map equivalent (accessible table/summary) so no information is map-only; meaningful alt on species images; ARIA live regions for async updates; respect prefers-reduced-motion.
A cross on R5 blocks merge (legal, PSBAR 2018); the accessibility statement (P6-S2) is on the critical path.
securityC2: Recorder1, BLISS, Eastings, Northings, Comments (withheld entirely), AND any per-record 'sensitivity' marker NEVER reach the public client — enforced by strict Zod schemas, a contract test, and a CI grep-guard on source + built bundle, every build (C2).

Sensitive-taxa precise coords generalised/suppressed SERVER-SIDE and BLENDED into the ordinary grid (indistinguishable, no marker) in what the endpoint RETURNS (A4); Place suppressed/coarsened for sensitive taxa (A4); low-count counts bucketed server-side (k-anonymity); a global precision floor applies (A5). Verified by e2e wire inspection AND re-proven against the real API at P6.5.

Client renders WGS84 honest cells at returned precision only — never raw BNG, never false-precision pins, never up-sampled; records gridRef precision == map precisionMetres; reprojection is server-side (A2).
No secrets/DB credentials/tokens/SQL in the client; VITE_* is public (API base + map style/tile URLs only); dev->prod is an API-env credential swap (R6/A12), proven config-only at P6.5/P7.

No client-assembled SQL; only whitelisted params leave the client; the server validates/whitelists all params (§12, flagged). HTTPS only; known-origin CORS; CSP compatible with MapLibre; sanitise any user-facing HTML.

A confirmed leak of PII/precise-sensitive-coords/sensitivity-marker is a HARD STOP: block merge, escalate to the team as a server-contract bug. A cross on C2 blocks merge. Client checks are nets; server enforcement (A3/A4/A5) is the fix.
performance: Code-split by route/feature; lazy-load the map + heavy libs; tree-shake; analyse the bundle; set + check payload/bundle budgets in CI.
Push data-heavy work server-side (tiles/aggregation/pagination); cap max zoom to meaningful precision; debounce viewport-driven fetches; simplify geometry.
Cache via TanStack Query (staleTime) + HTTP caching; virtualise long lists/tables; optimise images (dimensions/formats/lazy, attribution intact).
Measure Core Web Vitals (LCP/INP/CLS) on a modest-device/connection profile.
testing: Vitest + RTL for behaviour and accessible roles/names, not implementation details; cover lib/geo (grid-ref/precision) thoroughly.
Contract tests validate Zod parsing against fixtures AND assert PII/precise-coord/sensitivity fields are ABSENT and gridRef precision == precisionMetres (C2 regression guard).
Playwright E2E for core journeys including keyboard-only paths; @axe-core/playwright across routes; e2e network inspection for the C2 wire gate, RE-RUN against the real API at P6.5.
CI: typecheck + lint + unit + a11y + C2/secret grep-guard on every PR; no merge on red.
documentation: README (setup/env/run/build/deploy/embed/test) kept current; .env.example documents every dev+prod variable (values omitted).

ADRs (decision/why/alternatives/trade-offs) mirroring the Strategy decision log — stack/boundary, env/credential-swap, contract-first/Zod, design tokens, image attribution, map data-delivery, accessibility/attribution.
Comments explain WHY not WHAT, especially grid-ref/projection + sensitivity logic; handover runbook (prod PostgreSQL repoint config-only, tile/API config, publishing the accessibility statement).
Every non-trivial PR cites requirement ID(s) and appends the §17 Requirement Verification block; stale docs are treated as worse than none; docs current at merge time.

risks.description: Backend API + PostGIS (team-owned) not ready or contract diverges when the sole front-end owner needs it, stalling data-dependent phases and the P6.5 real-API C2 re-proof.
risks.mitigation: Develop against the MSW mock implementing the proposed apiContract (A11); Zod schemas are the single source of truth; integrate by swapping VITE_API_BASE_URL; converge the mock to the real contract early; STATE the date by which the real/staging API must exist for P6.5 to complete before showcase (Q14); if unavailable, fall back to a mock demo and flag as the top schedule risk; flag the dependency at mid-review.

risks.description: Sensitive-species precise coordinates, a sensitivity marker, or Recorder1/BLISS/Comments PII leak to the public client (C2 HARD STOP / GDPR breach).
risks.mitigation: Types + strict Zod exclude forbidden fields AND any 'sensitivity' marker; contract test + CI grep-guard fail on any excluded field; e2e network inspection verifies the wire and re-runs against the real API (P6.5); generalisation blended server-side (A4); precision floor + low-count bucketing + Place suppression server-side (A4/A5); any client-only mitigation is a contract bug to flag; a cross on C2 blocks merge.
risks.owner: Michael (client guards) + team API owner (server enforcement)

risks.description: Coarse grid records plotted as false-precision pins, client-side reprojection of BNG, or up-sampling a coarse ref, implying exact locations (scientific dishonesty + C2).
risks.mitigation: Grid/atlas occupancy polygons at true precision are the honest default; lib/geo helpers unit-tested against varying-precision fixtures and forbidden from up-sampling; records gridRef precision == precisionMetres; cap zoom to meaningful precision; no client BNG->WGS84; surface records!=populations + effort-bias caveats (P6).

risks.description: Map fails WCAG 2.2 AA (keyboard operability / non-map fallback) — an R5 legal build-blocker; canvas maps are a classic a11y gap.
risks.mitigation: Build the non-map accessible table in the same slice as the map (P2-S2); keyboard operability + labelled controls + deferred manual SR pass in P3-S3; axe + manual SR pass before merge; a cross on R5 blocks merge.

risks.description: Embedding method / parent-page CSP / tile host unknown (A7/A9), breaking MapLibre tile/style loading, routing, or focus management once embedded on BRERC's site.
risks.mitigation: Build routing + CSP behind config flags; confirm embed method + CSP + basemap host with Tim Corner/BRERC IT early (A7/A9/Q6); verify in a staging embed during P7.

risks.description: MapLibre + heavy libs blow the performance/bundle budget on modest devices; or error/telemetry logs leak sensitive detail.
risks.mitigation: Route-level lazy-load of the map + heavy libs; code-split; set/measure budgets in CI; push tiles/aggregation server-side; telemetry redacts by default and never receives PII.

risks.description: R8 internal dashboard pulls effort away from the public dashboard, breaching public-first (R1/C1); or a stray secret ships in a VITE_* var.
risks.mitigation: R8 is a single clearly-deferred phase (P8) gated on P7/R1-R7 DoD being green AND team assignment (A17); reduce to a handover note if unassigned; drop R8 before compromising R1-R7. Client knows only apiBaseUrl + public map URLs; .env.example documents VITE_* as PUBLIC; grep-guard + config validation catch stray secrets.

risks.description: Unattributed / mis-licensed species imagery or basemap (licence breach); or an assumed licence where the licence field is NULL.
risks.mitigation: Attribution is structured data required by the AttributedImage API — no image without licence + author + source (P2-S3/P4-S2); consolidated attributions page (P6-S3); never assume a licence where absent; confirm approved image source + basemap licence with Tim Corner (A7/A8).

risks.description: Scope creep from the brief's POTENTIAL outcomes treated as commitments, or product/UX/ethics/copy decisions (palette, caveat wording, statement text, geodiversity scope, group taxonomy) made silently in code.
risks.mitigation: Every phase/step cites a real requirement ID; candidates stay candidates until refined with BRERC; defer product/UX/copy to the Strategy prompt / Tim Corner; implement against flagged placeholders (A13/A14); open questions tracked (§15/C1).

risks.description: Fetch calls scatter into components (breaking the single-API-layer rule); or Dependabot PR churn (already active, incl. node-26 vs Node 25) + dependency drift overload the single owner in the tight P0 window.
risks.mitigation: Lint rule forbidding fetch outside lib/api + CI guard + review checklist; pin versions; Dependabot scoped/grouped/scheduled + Node pinned to 25 in P0-S1; npm audit in CI.

risks.description: Sparse ecological data makes empty results common; a naive UI reads empty as an error/bug.
risks.mitigation: First-class accessible empty state ('no records here' is meaningful, built in P0); test empty paths explicitly across every data view (§10).

risks.description: Single part-time front-end owner over-scopes and misses the mid-review demo or final DoD.
risks.mitigation: One deliberately-rough honest vertical SPIKE for mid-review (P2, DoD-exempt except C2/R5 core); tokens + primitives pulled into P0 so P2 assembles; stated hours budget (A15) back-solves phase sizes; named buffer + de-scope decision tree (timeline); MSW unblocks parallelism; small reviewable PRs; R8 hard-deferred (P8); scope refined with BRERC (C1).

risks.description: Front-end fixtures / Zod schemas drift from the eventual real API, causing late rework at the credential swap.
risks.mitigation: Keep fixtures strictly contract-shaped (A10/A11/A16); Zod schemas as single source; decide/document strict-vs-passthrough; share the proposed apiContract with the backend owner for early convergence; contract tests catch divergence; reconcile at the P6.5 integration gate.

risks.description: Map e2e tests flake, eroding CI trust.
risks.mitigation: Deterministic fixtures, stable selectors, mock tiles; retry-with-backoff only in the e2e harness, never the app.

phaseToMilestone.rationale: Foundations + guardrails + design tokens + primitives + green CI are the critical-path enabler; pulling tokens/primitives here makes P2 assembly. Started immediately on branch michael/phase-0-foundations.
The typed, PII/sensitivity-safe, Zod-validated api layer + MSW mock unblocks all UI work independent of the team backend (RK1).

The deliberately-rough honest vertical slice (one species: map + species + accessible fallback) IS the mid-review deliverable — concrete + honest for Tim Corner + Jaslyn Leong, with full DoD deferred to P3+.

phaseToMilestone.targetMilestone: midReview -> ~2026-08-03 (deepen the R2 centrepiece + clear P2 a11y debt)
phaseToMilestone.rationale: Deepen the R2 centrepiece (MVT, keyboard, wire-level C2) — the largest single risk (map a11y + honesty), given runway before final.
Species index + full detail + images round out the public R3 story; a self-contained slice; ON THE CRITICAL CHAIN into P6->P7.
Search/filters tie map + species + table into an explorable journey; de-scopable to species+year for the final presentation (deScopeDecisionTree).
Landing + LEGAL accessibility statement (pulled early, critical path) + provenance + attributions complete the public DoD surface.
Real-API integration + C2 re-proof against real responses; the one non-circular validation of the config-only swap and no-precise-coords claims (RK1/Q14).
Hardening, E2E/a11y automation, perf budgets, resilience, and the handover pack land the deliverable for showcase + BRERC handover.

phaseToMilestone.targetMilestone: only if slack remains before showcase (2026-08-17) AND internal-web/ assigned (A17)
phaseToMilestone.rationale: SECONDARY R8, strictly gated on R1-R7 DoD + assignment; likely out of scope for 8 weeks and dropped (or reduced to a handover note) before compromising the public dashboard.

buffers: A named integration/slippage buffer of ~2-3 working days (P6.5, ~2026-08-06..08) sits before the 2026-08-10 final presentation.
The accessibility statement (P6-S2) and the manual a11y checklist (P7-S1) are pulled as early as their dependencies allow because they legally gate merge (R5).
deScopeDecisionTree: IF P3 (hardest phase) overruns past ~2026-08-03: freeze P3 at 'GeoJSON+clustering + keyboard + wire-C2' depth and DEFER the MVT tile-source path (P3-S2) to post-showcase; GeoJSON alone satisfies R2 for the demo.

IF the schedule compresses before 2026-08-10: cut P5 to a single species+year filter (defer group/spatial/URL-sync); reduce P4 species-index breadth to the demo species set; NEVER cut P6-S2 (legal) or any C2/R5 gate.

IF the real/staging API is unavailable by ~2026-08-06 (RK1/Q14): keep the MSW demo, mark the P6.5 real-API C2 re-proof as an open handover item + top risk, and do NOT claim the config-only swap as verified.
R8 (P8) is the first thing dropped; it never displaces any R1-R7 DoD work.
timeline.criticalPathNote: P7 transitively depends on P3, P5, and P6; P6 depends on P4; P5 depends on P3+P4. The LONGEST chain into the final deliverable is P0->P1->P2->P4->P6->P6.5->P7 (carrying the LEGAL accessibility statement at P6-S2), which is the declared critical path. The parallel chain P0->P1->P2->P3->P5->P6.5->P7 runs alongside it; P3 (hardest phase) and P5 have limited slack, not zero — if either overruns, apply deScopeDecisionTree. Neither P4 nor P6 has slack (both are on the critical path), so P6-S2 is explicitly NOT treated as slack.

openQuestions.question: Which taxa/groups does BRERC classify as sensitive/protected; what generalisation resolution (e.g. 1km/10km) or suppression rule must the API apply; must Place be suppressed and low counts bucketed for these taxa before anything leaves the server?

Confirm the API strips Recorder1 and all recorder PII, WITHHOLDS Comments entirely, excludes BLISS + Eastings/Northings + any per-record sensitivity marker from every public response, and that dev-subset absence is not relied upon in production.

Does the server reproject EPSG:27700 -> EPSG:4326 and return honest grid-cell geometry + precision (not raw eastings/northings or false-precision points), using parameterised PostGIS queries? Confirm ownership.
What is the map data-delivery mode per volume — vector tiles (ST_AsMVT / pg_tileserv / Martin) vs GeoJSON — and what are the tile/style URLs and their timeline?
What basemap style/tiles are licensed for a public UK gov site, are they CSP-compatible without a client-held provider secret, and what attribution must render?
How will the SPA be embedded on BRERC's website (iframe vs mounted bundle vs sub-path), and what parent-page CSP / CORS / base-path / routing (hash vs history) constraints apply?
What is the approved species-image source and attribution policy (e.g. Wikipedia/Wikimedia CC BY-SA / PD), and does it return author + licence as structured data?
What temporal precision is acceptable for public filtering/display (exact dates vs year/YearEnd) given re-identification risk?
Is geodiversity (not only species) in the public scope; and is the 'group' browse/filter dimension derived from a taxonomy the team maintains — if so, what is the classification source + value set?
Confirm dev->prod is purely an API-environment credential swap with no client change, and provide the dev + prod API base + map/tile URLs for .env.example.
What is the priority order / must-haves among the brief's POTENTIAL outcomes, so scope is refined public-first with BRERC and nothing is over-built?

For R8 (if reached): who owns internal-web/, which internal endpoints and access controls exist for Recorder1/BLISS and data-quality flags, what audit/logging is required, and is it in scope within 8 weeks?
Is there a preferred client-error/telemetry destination, and confirm it must never receive PII/sensitive detail?
By what date will a real or staging API exist so the P6.5 real-API integration + C2 re-proof can complete before the 2026-08-17 showcase?
Can BRERC/the team share the actual dev subset export (main5.xls rows) so fixtures are derived from real data rather than the documented schema alone?

rules: requirements[] MUST contain exactly the ids R1,R2,R3,R4,R5,R6,R7,R8,C1,C2,C3.
Every phases[].requirementIds and phases[].steps[].requirementIds value MUST be a member of requirements[].id.
Every risks[].requirementIds, apiContract[].requirementIds, and openQuestions[].blocks value MUST be a member of requirements[].id.
For EVERY phase, union(steps[].requirementIds) MUST be a subset of that phase.requirementIds (phase-vs-step consistency).

validationContract.coverageMap MUST be machine-derived-equal to the actual coverage computed from phases: for each requirement id, coverageMap[id] == the set of phase ids where the id appears in phase.requirementIds OR any of its steps[].requirementIds. (This replaces the prior prose coverage line, eliminating drift.)
Every id in R1..R8 MUST appear in >=1 phase (via phase or step requirementIds), i.e. coverageMap[R1..R8] each non-empty.
R1 MUST NOT appear in P8 (public-first: the deferred R8 shell carries no public R1 work).
Each of C1,C2,C3 MUST appear in >=1 phases[].requirementIds or a crossCuttingGates entry.
phases[].dependsOn MUST reference only defined phase ids and MUST form a DAG (acyclic): P0<-[]; P1<-P0; P2<-P1; P3<-P2; P4<-P2; P5<-{P3,P4}; P6<-{P2,P4}; P6.5<-{P3,P5,P6}; P7<-P6.5; P8<-P7.

criticalPath entries MUST all be valid phase ids and form a linear suborder of the dependency DAG; criticalPath [P0,P1,P2,P4,P6,P6.5,P7] is the longest chain into the final deliverable and MUST include every phase P7 transitively depends on that lies on that longest chain (P4 and P6 included).

R8 MUST be priority=secondary and MUST appear only in the final phase P8, which MUST dependOn the phase carrying R1-R7 DoD (P7); P8 MUST come after every phase carrying R1-R7 in the ordered list and be gated on P7 exit criteria + team assignment (A17) (public-first).
P2 (thin vertical slice) MUST be mapped to the midReview milestone in timeline.phaseToMilestone and MUST be marked DoD-exempt except its C2 + R5-core hard gates.
A real-API integration phase (P6.5) MUST exist, MUST re-prove the C2 wire gates against real responses, and MUST verify the R6 dev->prod swap is config-only; its start is contingent on Q14/RK1.

No phases[].steps[] files[]/deliverables, and no apiContract[].responseShape/excludesFields, may surface Recorder1, BLISS, Eastings, Northings, Comments, or any 'sensitivity' marker to the client; apiContract[].excludesFields MUST include Recorder1, BLISS, Eastings, Northings, Comments, and sensitivity on every endpoint.

No phases[].steps[] may describe the client connecting to PostgreSQL, holding DB credentials, assembling SQL, reprojecting BNG client-side, up-sampling grid-ref precision, or placing a fetch/network call outside web/src/lib/api.

Every apiContract[] entry MUST define zodValidated and a non-empty excludesFields containing Recorder1/BLISS/Eastings/Northings/Comments/sensitivity (MVT excepted from runtime Zod but not from exclusion).

Every apiContract[] path-parameter identifier MUST resolve to a defined field: {speciesId} is the server-derived per-taxon id documented in groundTruthData (derived from Scientific_Name, A14) and appears in the species responseShape; no undefined identifier is used.
groundTruthData.fields MUST mark Eastings, Northings, Recorder1, BLISS, Comments with sensitivity == strip and Place/Grid_Ref with sensitivity == review, and none of the five strip fields as public.

Place MUST NOT appear in any public responseShape for sensitive taxa (suppressed server-side, A4); /api/records and /api/distribution/cells place is nullable + suppressed for sensitive taxa; MVT excludes place entirely.

Every assumptions[].id (A1..A17) and openQuestions[].id (Q1..Q15) referenced in text MUST exist in its list; every assumption touching API/data/BRERC-policy MUST name confirm_with and a status in {open,assumed}; every openQuestion MUST name blocks.

Every phase MUST have non-empty exitCriteria restating the relevant DoD gate; phases rendering data or UI MUST include the relevant C2 and/or R5 gate (P2 explicitly retains its C2 + R5-core hard gates despite the DoD spike exemption).

crossCuttingGates.accessibility MUST assert a cross on R5 blocks merge; crossCuttingGates.securityC2 MUST assert a cross on C2 blocks merge, that client env exposes only VITE_* non-secret values, that Comments are withheld entirely, and that no per-record 'sensitivity' marker reaches the public client.
